mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive momentum in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a robust and expanding economy, leading to higher corporate earnings expectations and positive equity returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest PAYEMS reading shows continued job growth.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in PAYEMS shows no statistically significant positive correlation with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 3%, it triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening by the Federal Reserve, increasing the discount rate for equities and causing negative forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Historical periods of high inflation often precede market downturns.
failure_condition: In periods where YoY CPIAUCSL is above 3%, the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not statistically different from periods where it is below 3% (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a low unemployment rate (UNRATE) and a rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals a late-cycle economy where the central bank is actively trying to curb growth to prevent overheating. This joint condition is bearish for equities.
evidence_refs: fact:The Fed has begun its tightening cycle as of 2017-04.
failure_condition: The strategy of shorting the S&P 500 when the trigger condition is met fails to produce a negative Sharpe ratio over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A strong positive inflection in industrial production (INDPRO) following a period of contraction indicates a cyclical recovery in the manufacturing and supply side of the economy, driving positive equity returns as activity resumes.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a key component of business cycle dating.
failure_condition: Instances of the trigger condition are not followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 55% of the time in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp, negative surprise in monthly retail sales (RSAFS) growth signals an unexpected weakening of consumer demand, which constitutes the largest part of the US economy. This negative signal prompts a rapid downward revision of growth expectations and negative short-term returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is approximately 70% of US GDP.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 1-month change in RSAFS and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is not consistently negative over rolling 3-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: A significant month-over-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) serves as a leading indicator of a slowdown in residential investment and broader economic activity, negatively impacting investor sentiment and equity market returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:The housing sector has historically been a strong leading indicator of recessions.
failure_condition: The average 60-day S&P 500 return following a trigger event is not statistically significantly lower than the unconditional average return.

mechanism_narrative: Stagflationary pressure, identified by concurrently rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) and falling industrial production (INDPRO), severely compresses corporate profit margins and increases economic uncertainty, leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The information coefficient of a signal based on this interaction does not remain below -0.02 in out-of-sample testing over a full economic cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP (GDPC1) growth is the most direct signal of a healthy, expanding economy. This fundamental strength translates into higher aggregate corporate earnings and supports higher equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 is the broadest measure of economic output.
failure_condition: The correlation between the quarter-over-quarter percent change in GDPC1 and the subsequent 60-day S&P 500 return is not statistically positive (p > 0.05) over the full sample period.
